purpose: Rank candidate mechanisms for axis-2 (non-tokenizer) component of r5 Φ 4-path FAIL.

H1_attention_topo.definition: Euclidean z-score distance over (heads, kv_heads, head_dim, depth, gqa_ratio).

H2_rope_with_scaling.definition: Pure rope log-ratio + 1 if rope_scaling mismatches.

H3_depth_width_topo.definition: Euclidean z-score distance over (depth, hidden, inter, ffn_ratio, depth/width).

H4_min_len.definition: Mean min(len_a,len_b) per prompt (low = many short prompts).

notes: p1_p2 is the most extreme FAIL (per write-up) in the sense that Variant A flips it sign-opposite to Variant B.
The diagnostic signal of interest: which hypothesis both (a) ranks p1_p2 high on the distance, and (b) separates r5 FAIL pairs {p1_p2,p2_p4,p3_p4} from PASS pairs.

verdict.top1_rationale: H2 (RoPE, rope_theta mismatch) is the ONLY hypothesis that can generate non-zero distance for p1_p2 (Qwen3-8B vs Llama-3.1-8B), because by every structural measure (H1 attention topology, H3 depth/width) Qwen3 and Llama-3.1 are near-identical (both GQA 32/8, head_dim=128; depth 36 vs 32; hidden 4096; SiLU). The only physical axis on which they differ substantively is rope_theta (1e6 vs 5e5) and rope_scaling (None vs llama3_factor8). Variant A (H/bpt) accidentally re-ranks pair spectra in a way that cancels the RoPE-induced Gram shift (rho(vA)=-0.49), consistent with RoPE acting as a multiplicative phase rotation that the bpt rescaling orthogonalizes.
verdict.top2_rationale: H1 has the best FAIL-vs-PASS separation (+1.07) — it correctly splits Gemma (p4) from the others (Qwen/Llama/Mistral share MHA geometry) — but fails on the p1_p2 discriminator (H1 predicts rank=1/6, i.e. MOST similar). H1 is useful for explaining why p2_p4 is the LARGEST failure (topology and rope BOTH diverge), but cannot uniquely explain p1_p2. Runner-up.

rejected.H3_depth_width_topo: Same failure mode as H1: p1_p2 rank=1/6 (predicts most similar). rho(r5)=+0.31 is moderate only.
rejected.H4_len_divergence: rho(r5)=-0.54 (wrong sign). Prompt-length is NOT the driver.
rejected.H4_min_len: rho(r5)=+0.44 modest but coincides with the p1_p2 rank=3/6 — not discriminating.

verdict.p1p2_anomaly_resolution: The direction-opposite response of p1_p2 to Variant A (H/bpt) and Variant B (H*bpt) is consistent with RoPE-induced phase rotation: multiplying or dividing the pooled last-token embedding by the path-specific bytes-per-token factor perturbs the ROTATION alignment between Qwen3 (theta=1e6) and Llama-3.1 (theta=5e5). Divide-by-bpt happens to COMPRESS the Llama embedding norm by a similar factor to what a theta=5e5→1e6 rotation-magnitude difference would accumulate over 4-10 token positions, accidentally restoring apparent alignment.

r6_recommendation.minimum_path_for_axis2: (A) 0-cost confirmation: swap p2 base model from Llama-3.1-8B to Qwen2.5-7B-Base (both GQA, same genealogy). Qwen2.5 uses rope_theta=1e6 (matches Qwen3). Predicted outcome: p1_p2 L2 drops below 0.147 WITHOUT any bpt re-projection. Zero GPU cost (we already have Qwen2.5-14B cached locally; retrain one LoRA adapter with the same corpus and the existing ALM pipeline).
r6_recommendation.alternative_A: (B) Pod-free: apply theoretical RoPE-correction at pool step: rotate the p2 pooled vector by the angular difference accumulated over (mean_token_pos=5) between theta_qwen=1e6 and theta_llama=5e5 on the FIRST few frequency bins of the head_dim=128 subspace. Since we only have the 256-d truncated hidden state, apply the rotation to the first 128 dims (which correspond to one attention head in the concatenated space) as a validation. Predicted: p1_p2 L2 drops 10-30%.
r6_recommendation.alternative_B: (C) r6 substrate-swap minimal: replace p2 with Qwen2.5-7B-Base OR replace p1 with Llama-3.2-3B-Base (whose rope_theta=500000 matches Llama-3.1). Either makes axis-2 vanish for the matched pair. SWAP p2 is more aligned with anima genealogy goals.
r6_recommendation.preferred: A (swap p2 to Qwen2.5-7B-Base); 0 new GPU, 1 LoRA retrain on existing pipeline.

methodology_note: All Spearman values computed on n=6 pairs (small sample). Interpretation is based on cross-hypothesis DIFFERENTIAL: H1/H3 give p1_p2 rank=1 (predict PASS), H2 gives p1_p2 rank=4 (predict elevated). This differential is robust even with n=6.
